metadata.title: Move Sync Controls to Top Bar

story.iWant: sync controls and last-sync status visible in the main top bar
story.soThat: I can run a sync and understand cache freshness without opening Settings
story.tasks: - Move manual-sync ownership from SettingsPage into MainPage/MainViewModel - Reuse ISyncManager range parameters for top-bar date-range sync - Add a persistent Sync button, relative last-sync label, exact tooltip, and in-progress state - Implement a light-dismiss flyout with start/end date pickers and inline validation - Remove manual sync controls from Settings and redirect empty-state prompts to the top-bar Sync flow - Extend MainViewModel and SettingsViewModel tests while keeping GoogleCalendarSyncTests as the sync-engine contract

acceptanceCriteria.ac: A persistent grey Sync button is visible in the main top bar on every calendar view.
The top bar shows a relative last-sync label that refreshes while the app is open.
If no successful sync exists, the top bar shows Never synced - click to sync.
Hovering the Sync button or label shows the exact last successful sync time in local time with timezone, or No sync on record if none exists.
Clicking Sync opens a light-dismiss date-range popdown below the button.
The popdown defaults to 6 months back and 1 month forward unless a visible-range prefill is supplied.
Confirm Sync runs the existing SyncManager pipeline for the selected range and shows an in-progress state.
Invalid ranges disable Confirm Sync and show inline validation.
After a successful sync, the top-bar label and tooltip update immediately.
SettingsPage no longer contains manual sync controls or last-sync UI.
The empty-state sync prompt points to the top-bar Sync flow and opens the same flyout with the visible range prefilled.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Epic 2 already defines the Google Calendar sync engine and the sync-status refresh pattern; Story 2.6 relocates the manual trigger into the shell rather than redefining sync behavior.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Provides original Epic 2 sequencing context, including the now-outdated settings-centric sync references that Story 2.6 must supersede in the live shell.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.section: Top Strip Controls and Tier 1 shell
doc.snippet: Supports keeping sync visibility in the always-available shell instead of burying it behind Settings.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Useful for package and platform guidance, but the live repo shape overrides the older multi-project structure shown there.

doc.title: Database Schemas
doc.snippet: Historical background for sync metadata. The live app uses data_source_refresh as the source for last successful sync time.

doc.title: Microsoft Learn - Flyout controls
doc.snippet: Confirms Flyout is the right transient container for arbitrary UI with outside-click and Escape dismissal.

doc.title: Microsoft Learn - Calendar date picker
doc.snippet: Confirms CalendarDatePicker is a calendar-overlay navigation input that should remain separate from the bounded sync-range selection flow.

file.reason: Already contains the top strip, Refresh Status button, empty-state card, and Settings entry point. Story 2.6 must attach the new sync cluster here.
Owns current shell interactions and is the most likely place to coordinate flyout opening from shell-level interactions.
Already owns sync-status refresh, LastSyncTooltip, view navigation, and SyncCompletedMessage handling. Extend it instead of creating a new sync toolbar view model.
Currently owns manual sync, cancellation, progress, and last-sync UI. Story 2.6 must remove that ownership without duplicating sync orchestration.
Still renders Sync with Google Calendar and related progress/last-sync controls that must be removed in this story.
Already supports explicit rangeStart and rangeEnd parameters. Reuse this existing contract for top-bar range sync.
Existing manual/background sync engine. Story 2.6 should call it, not fork it.
Authoritative source for last successful sync time used to drive the top-bar label and tooltip.
Already reads latest successful gcal refresh metadata from data_source_refresh.
MainViewModel already listens for this message. Reuse it instead of creating a competing completion signal.
Current shell-state test surface to extend for sync label formatting, validation, and range defaults.
Current sync/settings test surface to adjust once Settings becomes auth-only.
The existing sync-persistence integration contract should remain the authoritative SyncManager test harness.

constraints.constraint: Keep work in the existing root-level Views/ViewModels/Services/Messages/Data layout. Do not introduce the older Core/Data project split shown in planning docs.
After Story 2.6 there must be one manual-sync path in the shell. Do not leave a second independent sync orchestration flow in Settings.
Reuse ISyncManager.SyncAsync(rangeStart, rangeEnd, ...) and SyncCompletedMessage. Do not fork the Google sync engine for top-bar use.
Use ISyncStatusService.GetLastSyncTimeAsync() / data_source_refresh as the source of truth for the top-bar last-sync label and tooltip.
Use a Flyout-style light-dismiss popdown for the date-range picker. Do not use ContentDialog for this transient shell interaction.
Keep the existing CalendarDatePicker jump-to-date navigation affordance in the top bar.
SettingsPage remains the account/authentication surface after this story. Remove manual sync, progress, cancel, and last-sync UI from Settings.
Empty-state sync prompts must route to the same flyout and same MainViewModel-owned state, not a second prompt-specific sync path.
The worktree already contains in-progress shell edits. Developers must integrate with those changes and must not revert unrelated work.

tests.standards: xUnit + Moq + FluentAssertions. Prefer MainViewModel-focused unit tests for label formatting, default ranges, validation, and sync completion refresh. Keep GoogleCalendarSyncTests as the integration authority for SyncManager persistence.

ideas.test: No successful gcal refresh rows should produce Never synced - click to sync.
A recent successful sync should format the visible label as Last synced X minutes ago.
A sync older than 24 hours should switch to Last synced on [Day, Month D].
The hover tooltip should contain the exact local timestamp and timezone rather than the relative label text.
Opening the flyout without an explicit prefill should default to the standard range.
Start date after end date should produce the expected inline validation and disabled confirm state.
Receiving SyncCompletedMessage after a successful sync should refresh the label and tooltip immediately.
After the refactor, Settings should remain responsible for connect/reconnect only.
Range-based manual sync should continue to use the existing SyncManager persistence contract rather than a new path.
